mnemonics.title: SNMP Version Security
mnemonics.content: SNMP Security Gets Better Over Time: v1/v2c (Community Strings - Bad), v3 (Users & Encryption - Good).

mnemonics.title: SNMP Core Operations
mnemonics.content: GGSRT: Get, GetNext, Set, Response, Trap. Remember: 'Good Guys Send Reliable Telegrams'. v2c adds GETBULK and INFORM.

mnemonics.title: SNMP Architecture Components
mnemonics.content: MAMMO: Manager, Agent, Managed device, MIB, OID. The five pillars of SNMP architecture. Manager asks, Agent answers, MIB maps, OID identifies.

mnemonics.title: SNMP vs NETCONF Comparison
mnemonics.content: SNMP = Simple Monitoring, No-fuss Protocol (UDP, lightweight, monitoring focus). NETCONF = Network Enterprise Total Configuration Framework (SSH, heavy, config focus).

mnemonics.title: SNMPv3 Security Triple
mnemonics.content: AAE: Authentication (SHA/MD5), Authorization (VACM - View-based Access Control Model), Encryption (AES/DES). v3 has all three; v1/v2c have none.
